_scenario.title: Software vendor maps a manufacturer SKU to the neutral identity layer
_scenario.note: Registry data model per the MH Taxonomy Standard (naming layer, §4.9/§4.11). All identifiers are illustrative; no such registry records exist yet. Names point at identities — never the reverse (rules P3/D-8; Explorer rejects name-driven classification with E-102). See docs/demo-scenarios.md.

naming_layer_record.classification_authority: NONE — naming-layer records never influence classification

cross_reference_note: A competitor part with canonically matching geometry resolves to the SAME gsid through its own MPN → CMP chain; geometric equivalence is computed from the registry, not asserted from brand tables.
explanations: The SKU is recorded as a naming-layer pointer (MPN-018233 → CMP-000482); it carries zero classification authority (P3, D-8).
Classification came from what the part IS (role + section topology + configuration), established once from the manufacturer's specification.
A future rename is a one-row naming-layer update; the component, geometry, and every downstream cross-reference are untouched.

refusals.message: Classification from the SKU string alone was refused; manufacturer names are never classification inputs.
